name: Catalog Royalty Forensics (CRF) v1
description: Cross-document royalty statement reconstruction from license riders, amendments, finance memos, and sales ledgers. Solvers must compute exact quarter-end royalty outputs under public recoupment and reserve rules.

closest_existing_evals.name: Reimbursement Forensics (BenchBench)
closest_existing_evals.why_close: both require reconciling messy public evidence across several documents into exact numeric outputs under explicit policy rules
closest_existing_evals.why_not_duplicate: this benchmark is royalty-accounting rather than travel-policy accounting; the core difficulty is contract amendment precedence, territory and channel rights, reserve withholding, and advance recoupment

closest_existing_evals.name: Doc-style forensic reasoning benchmarks
closest_existing_evals.why_close: shared emphasis on document interpretation rather than pure puzzle solving
closest_existing_evals.why_not_duplicate: answers are deterministic ledger calculations with public contract mechanics, not open-ended extraction or subjective judgment

determinism_notes: Items are procedurally generated from a seed, and all answer-determining rules are solver-visible in the public rulebook and item documents.
